title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers and a target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target. Exactly one solution exists; you may not reuse an element.
constraints: 2 <= nums.length <= 1e4; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9; exactly one valid answer.

title: Merge Sorted Array
description: You are given two sorted integer arrays. The first has length m+n, with only the first m entries meaningful and the rest zero-filled padding; the second has length n and is fully populated. Merge the second array into the first in place so the first array becomes one sorted array of length m+n, then return it.
constraints: nums1.length == m+n; nums2.length == n; 0 <= m, n <= 200; -1e9 <= nums1[i], nums2[j] <= 1e9.

title: Remove Element
description: Given an array of integers and a value, remove every occurrence of that value in place, keeping the relative order of the remaining elements irrelevant, and return the count of elements that are not equal to the value.

title: Majority Element
description: Given an array of integers, return the value that appears in more than half of the elements. It is guaranteed such an element exists.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 5e4; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9; a majority element always exists.

title: Rotate Array
description: Given an integer array, rotate it to the right by k steps (k can exceed the array length) and return the rotated array.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given daily stock prices, choose one day to buy and a later day to sell to maximize profit, and return that maximum profit (0 if no profit is possible).
constraints: 1 <= prices.length <= 1e5; 0 <= prices[i] <= 1e4.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
description: Given daily stock prices, you may buy and sell as many times as you like (holding at most one share at a time). Return the maximum total profit achievable.
constraints: 1 <= prices.length <= 3e4; 0 <= prices[i] <= 1e4.

title: Jump Game
description: Each element of an array is the maximum jump length from that position. Starting at index 0, determine whether the last index is reachable.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 1e4; 0 <= nums[i] <= 1e5.

title: Jump Game II
description: Each element of an array is the maximum jump length from that position, and the last index is always reachable. Return the minimum number of jumps needed to reach the last index starting from index 0.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 1e4; 0 <= nums[i] <= 1000; last index always reachable.

title: H-Index
description: Given the citation counts for a researcher's papers, compute the h-index: the largest value h such that the researcher has at least h papers with at least h citations each.
constraints: 1 <= citations.length <= 5000; 0 <= citations[i] <= 1000.

title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
description: Design a data structure supporting average O(1) insert(val), remove(val), and getRandom() (uniformly random among current elements). Given a sequence of operations and their arguments, return the sequence of results (insert/remove return whether the set changed; getRandom returns an element currently present).
constraints: -2^31 <= val <= 2^31-1; at most 2e5 calls total; getRandom is called only when the structure is non-empty.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array, return an array where each element is the product of all the other elements (not itself), without using division and in O(n) time.
constraints: 2 <= nums.length <= 1e5; -30 <= nums[i] <= 30; product of any prefix/suffix fits in a 32-bit signed integer.

title: Gas Station
description: There are gas stations in a circle, each with a gas amount and a cost to travel to the next station. Starting with an empty tank, find the index of the station to start from to complete the full circuit, or return -1 if impossible. A solution, if it exists, is unique.
constraints: gas.length == cost.length; 1 <= gas.length <= 1e5; 0 <= gas[i], cost[i] <= 1e4.

title: Candy
description: Children stand in a line, each with a rating. Every child gets at least one candy, and a child with a strictly higher rating than either neighbor must get more candy than that neighbor. Return the minimum total candies needed.
constraints: 1 <= ratings.length <= 2e4; 0 <= ratings[i] <= 2e4.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given an elevation map represented by non-negative bar heights of width 1, compute how much rainwater it can trap after raining.
constraints: 1 <= height.length <= 2e4; 0 <= height[i] <= 1e5.

title: Roman to Integer
description: Convert a Roman numeral string (using symbols I, V, X, L, C, D, M with standard subtractive notation) to its integer value.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 15; s is a valid Roman numeral in the range [1, 3999].

title: Integer to Roman
description: Convert an integer in the range [1, 3999] into its Roman numeral representation using standard subtractive notation.

title: Length of Last Word
description: Given a string of words separated by spaces, return the length of the last word (a word is a maximal run of non-space characters).
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; s consists of English letters and spaces; s contains at least one non-space character.

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Given an array of strings, find the longest prefix common to all of them. Return an empty string if none exists.
constraints: 1 <= strs.length <= 200; 0 <= strs[i].length <= 200; strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.

title: Reverse Words in a String
description: Given a string, reverse the order of the words (maximal runs of non-space characters), collapsing any leading, trailing, or multiple in-between spaces to a single space between words.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; s contains English letters, spaces, and digits; s has at least one non-space character.

title: Zigzag Conversion
description: Write a string in a zigzag pattern across a given number of rows (top to bottom, then diagonally back up, repeating), then read the characters row by row and return the resulting string.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1000; 1 <= numRows <= 1000; s consists of English letters, commas, and periods.

title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String
description: Given a haystack string and a needle string, return the index of the first occurrence of the needle in the haystack, or -1 if the needle does not occur.
constraints: 1 <= haystack.length, needle.length <= 1e4; both consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Text Justification
description: Given a list of words and a line width, greedily pack words onto lines, then space-pad each line so left and right edges are flush (extra spaces distributed as evenly as possible, leftmost gaps get any remainder). The last line, and any line with a single word, is left-justified with trailing spaces to fill the width.
constraints: 1 <= words.length <= 300; 1 <= words[i].length <= 20; 1 <= maxWidth <= 100; words[i] consists of English letters and no spaces.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string, determine whether it reads the same forwards and backwards after lowercasing and removing every character that is not a letter or digit.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 2e5; s consists of printable ASCII characters.

input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Is Subsequence
description: Given strings s and t, determine whether s can be formed by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from t without reordering the remaining characters.
constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 100; 0 <= t.length <= 1e4; both consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description: Given a 1-indexed array sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers that add up to a target and return their 1-indexed positions as [index1, index2] with index1 < index2. Exactly one solution exists.
constraints: 2 <= numbers.length <= 3e4; -1000 <= numbers[i] <= 1000; numbers is sorted non-decreasing; exactly one solution exists.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given heights of vertical lines at each index, choose two lines that together with the x-axis form a container holding the most water, and return that maximum area.
constraints: 2 <= height.length <= 1e5; 0 <= height[i] <= 1e4.

title: 3Sum
description: Given an integer array, return all unique triplets [a,b,c] (as a list of index-independent triplets, not indices) with a+b+c == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.
constraints: 3 <= nums.length <= 3000; -1e5 <= nums[i] <= 1e5.

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array of positive integers and a target sum, find the length of the shortest contiguous subarray whose sum is at least the target. Return 0 if no such subarray exists.
constraints: 1 <= target <= 1e9; 1 <= nums.length <= 1e5; 1 <= nums[i] <= 1e4.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string, find the length of the longest contiguous substring that contains no repeated characters.
constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 5e4; s consists of English letters, digits, symbols, and spaces.

title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
description: Given a string s and an array of equal-length words, find every starting index in s where a substring is exactly a concatenation, in any order, of each word in the array exactly once (with no extra characters in between).
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; 1 <= words.length <= 5000; 1 <= words[i].length <= 30; all words have the same length; s and words[i] consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given strings s and t, find the shortest contiguous substring of s that contains every character of t (including duplicates) at least as many times as it appears in t. Return an empty string if no such window exists.
constraints: 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 1e5; s and t consist of English letters.

title: Valid Sudoku
description: Given a 9x9 board where 0 marks an empty cell, determine whether the currently filled cells satisfy Sudoku rules: no repeated digit 1-9 in any row, column, or 3x3 box (empty cells are ignored and the board need not be solvable/complete).

examples.input: a partially filled 9x9 board with no row/column/box conflicts

constraints: board.length == board[i].length == 9; board[i][j] is 0 (empty) or 1-9.

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix, return all its elements visited in spiral order starting from the top-left, going right, then down, then left, then up, spiraling inward.

title: Rotate Image
description: Given an n x n matrix representing an image, rotate it 90 degrees clockwise in place and return it.
constraints: n == matrix.length == matrix[i].length; 1 <= n <= 20; -1000 <= matrix[i][j] <= 1000.

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given an m x n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0, in place, and return the modified matrix.

title: Game of Life
description: Given a board of 0 (dead) and 1 (live) cells, compute the next generation under Conway's rules (a live cell with 2-3 live neighbors survives, a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive, all others die/stay dead) simultaneously for every cell, and return the updated board.
constraints: m == board.length; n == board[i].length; 1 <= m, n <= 25; board[i][j] is 0 or 1.

title: Ransom Note
description: Given two strings ransomNote and magazine, determine whether ransomNote can be built by cutting out and rearranging individual letters taken from magazine, where each letter in magazine may be used at most once.
constraints: 1 <= ransomNote.length, magazine.length <= 1e5; both strings consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Isomorphic Strings
description: Given two strings s and t of the same length, determine whether s can be transformed into t by a consistent one-to-one character substitution (every occurrence of a character in s maps to exactly one character in t, and no two characters map to the same target character).
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 5e4; t.length == s.length; s and t consist of any valid ASCII characters.

title: Word Pattern
description: Given a pattern string and a space-separated string s, determine whether s follows the same pattern, i.e. there exists a consistent one-to-one mapping between each character of pattern and each whitespace-delimited word of s.
constraints: 1 <= pattern.length <= 300; pattern contains only lowercase letters; 1 <= s.length <= 3000; s consists of lowercase letters and single spaces.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, determine whether t is an anagram of s (uses exactly the same multiset of characters, rearranged).
constraints: 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 5e4; s and t consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of lowercase strings, group the strings that are anagrams of each other. For this harness (no 2D string array type available yet), encode the result as an array of strings: each output string is one anagram group with its members joined by '|', the members of a group sorted alphabetically, and the groups themselves sorted alphabetically by their joined form.

examples.explanation: Groups sorted alphabetically; within each group members sorted alphabetically.

constraints: 1 <= strs.length <= 1e4; 0 <= strs[i].length <= 100; strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.

title: Happy Number
description: A number is 'happy' if repeatedly replacing it with the sum of the squares of its decimal digits eventually reaches 1 (rather than looping forever in a cycle that never includes 1). Given a positive integer n, determine whether it is happy.

title: Contains Duplicate II
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, determine whether there are two distinct indices i and j such that nums[i] == nums[j] and the absolute difference between i and j is at most k.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 1e5; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9; 0 <= k <= 1e5.

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted integer array nums, return the length of the longest run of consecutive integers (values that form an unbroken sequence, in any order in the array) that can be formed using nums' elements. Must run in O(n) time.
constraints: 0 <= nums.length <= 1e5; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9.

title: Summary Ranges
description: Given a sorted array of distinct integers, return the smallest list of maximal ranges (as strings) that exactly covers every value in the array: a single value x is written as "x", and a run from a to b (a < b) is written as "a->b".
constraints: 0 <= nums.length <= 20; -2^31 <= nums[i] <= 2^31 - 1; nums is sorted in strictly increasing order.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals [start, end], merge all intervals that overlap and return the resulting set of non-overlapping intervals, sorted by start.
constraints: 1 <= intervals.length <= 1e4; intervals[i].length == 2; 0 <= starti <= endi <= 1e4.

title: Insert Interval
description: Given a list of non-overlapping intervals already sorted by start, and a new interval, insert the new interval into the list (merging with any overlapping intervals as needed) and return the resulting sorted, non-overlapping list of intervals.
constraints: 0 <= intervals.length <= 1e4; intervals is sorted by starti with no overlaps; newInterval.length == 2.

title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
description: Balloons are given as intervals [xstart, xend] on the x-axis. An arrow shot straight up at position x bursts every balloon whose interval contains x. Return the minimum number of arrows needed to burst every balloon.
constraints: 1 <= points.length <= 1e5; points[i].length == 2; -2^31 <= xstart <= xend <= 2^31 - 1.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing only the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine whether every bracket is closed by the matching type of bracket, and closed in the correct order.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; s consists only of bracket characters '()[]{}'.

title: Simplify Path
description: Given an absolute Unix-style file path, convert it to its simplified canonical form: collapse consecutive slashes, resolve '.' (current directory, drop it) and '..' (parent directory, pop the last component, never rising above root), and join the remaining components with single slashes, starting with '/' and never ending with a trailing slash unless the result is the root.
constraints: 1 <= path.length <= 3000; path is a valid absolute Unix path made of printable ASCII characters.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack supporting push(val), pop(), top(), and getMin() (current minimum element), each in O(1) time. Given a sequence of operations and their arguments, return the sequence of results (push/pop return nothing meaningful -> represented as null; top/getMin return the value).
constraints: -2^31 <= val <= 2^31 - 1; pop/top/getMin are called only on a non-empty stack; at most 3e4 calls total.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate an arithmetic expression given as tokens in Reverse Polish (postfix) Notation. Valid operators are +, -, *, / (integer division truncates toward zero). Return the integer result.
constraints: 1 <= tokens.length <= 1e4; each token is an integer in [-200,200] or one of + - * /; the expression is always valid and never divides by zero.

title: Basic Calculator
description: Given a string expression containing non-negative integers, the operators '+' and '-', parentheses '(' ')', and spaces, evaluate the expression (respecting parentheses and left-to-right associativity, no multiplication/division) and return the integer result.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 3e5; s consists of digits, '+', '-', '(', ')', and ' '; the expression is valid and its result fits in a 32-bit integer.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, determine whether the list contains a cycle (some node's next pointer, followed repeatedly, eventually revisits a node already seen). For testing, a cycle is described by pos: the 0-indexed position in the list that the tail connects back to, or -1 if there is no cycle.
constraints: the number of nodes is in [0, 1e4]; -1e5 <= Node.val <= 1e5; pos is -1 or a valid index into the list.

title: Add Two Numbers
description: Given two non-empty linked lists each representing a non-negative integer with digits stored in reverse order (one digit per node), add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list in the same reverse-digit format.
constraints: the number of nodes in each list is in [1, 100]; 0 <= Node.val <= 9; no leading zero except the number 0 itself.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Given the heads of two sorted linked lists, merge them into one sorted list by splicing together their nodes and return the head of the merged list.
constraints: the number of nodes in each list is in [0, 50]; -100 <= Node.val <= 100; both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
description: Given the head of a linked list where each node additionally has a random pointer that may point to any node in the list (or null), build and return a deep copy of the list. For this harness each node is encoded as a pair [val, randomIndex] where randomIndex is the 0-indexed position of the node its random pointer targets, or null.
constraints: the number of nodes is in [0, 1000]; -1e4 <= Node.val <= 1e4; Node.random is null or points to a node in the list.

title: Reverse Linked List II
description: Given the head of a singly linked list and two 1-indexed positions left and right, reverse the nodes strictly between positions left and right (inclusive) in a single pass and return the resulting head.
constraints: the number of nodes is n; 1 <= n <= 500; -500 <= Node.val <= 500; 1 <= left <= right <= n.

title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list k at a time and return the resulting head. If the number of remaining nodes is not a multiple of k, leave the final short group as-is (unreversed). Nodes themselves must be relinked, not just their values swapped.
constraints: the number of nodes is n; 1 <= n <= 5000; 0 <= Node.val <= 1000; 1 <= k <= n.

title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the n-th node counting from the end of the list (1-indexed) and return the resulting head, in a single pass.
constraints: the number of nodes is sz; 1 <= sz <= 30; 0 <= Node.val <= 100; 1 <= n <= sz.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
description: Given the head of a sorted linked list, delete every node that has at least one duplicate value, leaving only distinct-valued nodes from the original sorted list, and return the resulting head.
constraints: the number of nodes is in [0, 300]; -100 <= Node.val <= 100; the list is sorted in non-decreasing order.

title: Rotate List
description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer k, rotate the list to the right by k places (each rotation moves the last node to the front) and return the resulting head.
constraints: the number of nodes is in [0, 500]; -100 <= Node.val <= 100; 0 <= k <= 2*10^9.

title: Partition List
description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer x, rearrange the nodes so that all nodes with value less than x come before all nodes with value greater than or equal to x, preserving the original relative order within each partition, and return the resulting head.
constraints: the number of nodes is in [0, 200]; -100 <= Node.val <= 100; -200 <= x <= 200.

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache with a fixed positive capacity, supporting get(key) (return the value or -1 if absent, marking it most recently used) and put(key, value) (insert or update, marking it most recently used, evicting the least recently used entry if capacity is exceeded), both in O(1) average time. Given a sequence of operations and their arguments (the first operation constructs the cache with its capacity), return the sequence of results (put and the constructor return null).
constraints: 1 <= capacity <= 3000; 0 <= key, value <= 1e4; at most 2e5 calls total to get and put.
